item.title: Migrant workers fuel economy at high cost: lawmaker Rumeen
item.description: More than one crore Bangladeshis working abroad have become a major pillar of the country’s economy, but their contribution comes at a heavy human cost, independent lawmaker Rumeen Farhana told the Jatiya Sangsad on Tuesday....

item.title: Gen Z protesters return to Nepal streets
item.description: Nepal’s prime minister Balen Shah, who rose to power on the back of a Gen Z-driven political movement, now finds himself at the centre of a storm led by the very generation, reports India Today....News

item.title: Ukraine civilian deaths hit four-year high in June: UN
item.description: More civilians were killed in Ukraine in June than in any single month since April 2022, the United Nations said Tuesday, as Russia escalated long-range missile strikes....

item.title: ‘Case business’ derails July massacre trial: Nahid
item.description: National Citizen Party convener Nahid Islam, also the opposition chief whip, on Tuesday alleged that the trial process in the July massacre cases was being derailed by a ‘case business’....

item.title: Early menopause hits 1 in 13 Bangladeshi women: study
item.description: About one in every 13 Bangladeshi women experiences menopause before the age of 45, according to the largest multi-country study on the issue led by researchers at the International Centre for Diarrhoeal Disease Research, Bangladesh....

item.title: Impractical training behind unskilled workers: experts
item.description: The skills training system of Bangladesh has failed to produce competent workers or ensure decent employment at home and abroad, leaving lakhs of young people discouraged despite hundreds of government and private training institutes operating across the country....ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Indictment hearing against Hasina, 285 others postponed to Sept 30
item.description: The Dhaka 3rd Additional Metropolitan Sessions Judge Court on Tuesday adjourned till September 30 the hearing on charge framing in a sedition case filed against ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina and 285 others....

item.title: Fresh US-Iran fighting rages
item.description: The United States launched a fresh wave of strikes on Iran on Tuesday after president Donald Trump vowed to reimpose a naval blockade, prompting Tehran to respond with strikes on targets in countries around the region....

item.title: FRESH IMF LOAN: Govt plans to tap int’l bond market
item.description: The government is considering tapping the international bond market to meet its growing foreign currency needs as borrowing from multilateral lenders becomes more expensive following interest rate hikes, officials said on Tuesday....

item.title: FY27 export target ambitious: experts
item.description: Industry leaders said that the proposed $66 billion export target of Bangladesh for the 2026-27 financial year was ambitious amid the ongoing global business and geopolitical atmosphere......Country-specificreports

item.title: PM urges youths to embrace entrepreneurship
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Tuesday called on young people to pursue entrepreneurship despite challenges ahead, assuring them that the government would provide support and guidance to help turn innovative ideas into successful businesses......

item.title: No change in our approach, India says on Hasina’s return
item.description: Indian external affairs ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said on Tuesday that any extradition matter was a legal issue, and it would be dealt with accordingly,...

item.title: Shinepukur allowed to open import LCs thru Sonali Bank
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has allowed Shinepukur Ceramics PLC, a company of the default-stricken Beximco Group, to open import letters of credit for raw materials by granting it a temporary exemption from a key provision of the Bank Company Act, aiming to keep the factory operating and protect employment......

item.title: Bangladesh now has 290 LEED-certified RMG factories
item.description: The country now has 290 Energy and Environmental Design (LEED)-certified garment factories, with four new factories recently earning LEED certifications from the United States Green Building Council......GeographicReference

item.title: BSEC allows intraday trading
item.description: In a major move to modernise the capital market, Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission has decided to provide consent to the stock exchanges to introduce ‘Script Netting’ or ‘Intraday Trading’......

item.title: Bangladesh, KSA discuss logistics ties
item.description: Bangladesh and Saudi Arabia on Tuesday held high-level bilateral meetings to further expand cooperation in the transport, logistics and port development sectors......

item.title: Blinded by bullets, almost forgotten by state
item.description: On her 21st birthday on July 3, Noorjahan Akhter did not think about cake, gifts or celebrations. Instead, she reflected on the tumultuous time of the July uprising two years earlier – the day she joined thousands of others in a movement to end Sheikh Hasina’s rule of injustice and discrimination, and lost her sight in one eye to a bullet....

item.title: DCCI urges stronger trade diplomacy, faster FTAs
item.description: Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry president Taskeen Ahmed on Tuesday urged the government to accelerate the signing of Free Trade Agreements and Preferential Trade Agreements with key trading partners and strengthen economic diplomacy to help Bangladesh remain competitive after its graduation from the Least Developed Country category......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Titumir seeks stronger global support to achieve SDGs
item.description: Rashed Al Mahmud Titumir, prime minister’s adviser on the ministries of Finance and Planning, on Tuesday called for stronger international solidarity, increased concessional financing and reforms to the global financial architecture to help the Least Developed Countries achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and ensure smooth graduation from the LDC category......

item.title: A ministry intransigence that weighs too heavily on students
item.description: THOUSANDS of students taking the higher secondary and equivalent examinations had to endure risky travel to examination centres amidst flooding, heavy downpour and submerged roads on July 13. The students took the examinations in inclement weather also on July 11 and they will take the examinations on July 15, with more rain forecast. Whilst they waded through......

item.title: Govt needs to move beyond policy on mental health care
item.description: THAT more than 92 per cent of people living with mental health conditions receive no treatment is alarming. The health minister said in the parliament that around 16.8 per cent of adults and 12.6 per cent of children and adolescents suffer from one or more mental health conditions according to the National Mental Health Survey 2018–19. The minister has also noted that......

item.title: Skills for a shared future
item.description: EACH year on July 15, the world observes World Youth Skills Day to highlight the importance of equipping young people with the knowledge and competencies needed for decent work and sustainable development. The 2026 theme, ‘Skills for a shared future: Empowering youth with future-ready skills for inclusive, peaceful and sustainable societies’, reflects a reality that......

item.title: Revitalising dormant industries
item.description: OVER the past decade, Bangladesh has achieved remarkable progress in infrastructure development, export growth and key social indicators. Yet, a significant contradiction remains within the industrial sector: while new industries continue to emerge, many existing factories and industrial enterprises remain closed, idle or only partially operational. Reviving these......

item.title: When the rain is not the disaster
item.description: WHEN the Chattogram Meteorological Office recorded 412.3 millimetres of rain in just 24 hours this week — the figures understandably dominated headlines. News reports framed the event as an act of nature: a low-pressure system lingering over the Bay of Bengal, an unusually wet spell after a dry start to Ashar, and a city overwhelmed by exceptional rainfall. None of......

item.title: Is Marx’s ghost still haunting White House?
item.description: MARKING the 250th anniversary of American independence at Mount Rushmore, President Donald Trump weaponised a traditional celebration of unity into a platform for ideological hostility and electoral mobilisation. His repeated invocation of terms such as ‘communist menace’, ‘Marxists’, ‘newcomers’, ‘enemies of America’, ‘enemy of the constitution’, ‘vanquish......

item.title: Deschamps says France 'devastated' by defeat, questions referee
item.description: France coach Didier Deschamps said his players were left ‘devastated’ by their World Cup semi-final defeat to Spain on Tuesday before aiming a barb at match referee Ivan Barton from El Salvador....News

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, reimposes naval blockade
item.description: US forces carried out fresh strikes against Iran for a fourth day in a row on Tuesday and reimposed a naval blockade to prevent ships from sailing to or from Iran ......

item.title: Majestic Spain stun France to reach World Cup final
item.description:
Spain stunned tournament favourites France 2-0 to reach the World Cup final on Tuesday, producing a tactical masterpiece to shatter Les Bleus' dreams of winning the title for the third time.
...

item.title: Dhaka premiere of ‘Mastul’ held
item.description: Award-winning filmmaker Mohammad Nuruzzaman’s second film ‘Mastul,’ premiered at the Russian House in the capital on Tuesday....News

item.title: DU Alumni 99-00 launches Silver Jubilee web site
item.description: Preparations for the Silver Jubilee celebration of Dhaka University Alumni 99-00, the platform for alumni of the university’s 1999-2000 academic session, have officially begun with the launch of a dedicated web site and registration process....

item.title: Students announce long march to edn ministry for today
item.description: Students have announced a ‘long march to the education ministry’ on Wednesday afternoon to press their demand for the resignation of education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque MilonANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon....Soccer

item.title: Students announce long march to edn ministry for Wednesday
item.description: Students have announced a ‘long march to the education ministry’ on Wednesday afternoon to press their demand for the resignation of education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque MilonANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon....

item.title: 27 forest pirates surrender in Sundarban
item.description: Twenty-seven members of the notorious Chhoto Jahangir pirate gang, including its prime leader Jahangir Sheikh, surrendered to the Bangladesh Coast Guard in Sundarban, laying down a large cache of arms and ammunition in a significant success under the government’s anti-piracy campaign....

item.title: BMU seminar stresses early detection of liver cancer
item.description: Experts on Tuesday stressed the need for preventing liver cancer through early detection, modern treatment, liver transplantation and a national disease registry at a scientific seminar held ahead of World Hepatitis Day 2026....ExecutiveBranch

item.title: SAU hosts career guidance prog for students
item.description: Sher-e-Bangla Agricultural University on Tuesday organised a career guidance session titled ‘Empowering Youth-Season 2’ to help students develop skills, plan their careers and improve employability....
